name: Classic Mandelbrot Set
summary: The quintessential escape-time fractal. Points in the complex plane are tested for escape under repeated squaring. The boundary contains infinite detail.
notes: Rendered with a bailout of 500 and exposure target of 50 using a log1p-mapped shader to reveal structure near the cardioid and period bulbs.

name: Julia Set
summary: A connected Julia set for a parameter inside the Mandelbrot set. Shows dendritic filaments and spiral structure.
notes: Fixed c = -0.75 + 0.156i gives a dendrite-like shape. Distance field shading highlights filament width.

name: Burning Ship
summary: A variant that takes the absolute value of real and imaginary parts before squaring, producing fractal 'smoke' and ship-like shapes.
notes: Default viewport spans nearly the full Burning Ship set. Smoothed distance estimate avoids banding.

name: Tricorn (Mandelbar)
summary: The conjugate of the Mandelbrot iteration. It has a self-similar anti-holomorphic structure with three-fold symmetries.
notes: Standard viewport centered at origin. Exposure shading reveals the central bulb and satellite copies.

name: Multibrot-3
summary: The degree-3 analogue of the Mandelbrot set. The main cardioid becomes a reentrant shape with three-fold symmetry.
notes: Higher-power Mandelbrots have thinner filaments. A bailout of 500 captures enough orbit detail for clean shading.

name: Newton Fractal (z^3 - 1)
summary: Basins of attraction for Newton's method applied to z^3 - 1 = 0. The three basins meet at the Julia set of the method.
notes: Convergence tolerance set to 1e-8. Iteration count maps nicely to exposure; distance field emphasizes basin boundaries.

name: Glynn Fractal
summary: A complex-analytic fractal with fractional exponent 1.5. Produces a tree-like structure with dense branching.
notes: Viewport centered on the main tree. Logarithmic distance shading highlights fine branch tips.

name: Logistic Map
summary: A one-dimensional chaotic map plotted in the (r, x) plane. Classic example of period-doubling route to chaos.
notes: Full bifurcation diagram from r=2 to r=4. High exposure target captures transient chaotic bands.
